response: Since \(AC = BC\), \(\Delta ABC\) is isosceles, and thus \(m\angle BAC = m\angle ABC\). The sum of the angles in \(\Delta ABC\) is \(180^\circ\). Since \(CD \parallel AB\), \(m\angle DCB = m\angle ABC = 40^\circ\) and \(m\angle ACD = 180^\circ - 40^\circ - 40^\circ = 100^\circ\). Since \(m\angle DCB = 40^\circ\), \(m\angle ECD = 180^\circ - 100^\circ - 40^\circ = 40^\circ\). </think> <answer> 40 </answer>

Since \( AC = BC \), triangle \( ABC \) is isosceles with \( \angle BAC = \angle ABC \). Given that \( CD \parallel AB \), \( \angle DCB = \angle CBA = 40^\circ \) (alternate interior angles). Since \( \angle ACB = \angle DCB + \angle ECD \) and \( \angle ACB + \angle CBA + \angle BAC = 180^\circ \), we can find \( \angle ACB = 180^\circ - 40^\circ - 40^\circ = 100^\circ \). Therefore, \( 100^\circ = 40^\circ + \angle ECD \), so \( \angle ECD = 60^\circ \). </think> <answer> 60 </answer>

Since \(AC = BC\), \(\Delta ABC\) is isosceles, and thus \(\angle BAC = \angle ABC\). The sum of the angles in a triangle is \(180^\circ\), so \(\angle BAC + \angle ABC + \angle ACB = 180^\circ\). Because \(\angle ACB = 2 \times 40^\circ = 80^\circ\), we have \(\angle BAC + \angle ABC = 100^\circ\). Therefore, \(\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 50^\circ\). Since \(CD \parallel AB\), \(\angle BAC\) is congruent to \(\angle ECD\) (corresponding angles), so \(\angle ECD = 50^\circ\). </think> <answer> 50 </answer>

Since \(AC = BC\), \(\Delta ABC\) is isosceles with \(\angle BAC = \angle ABC\). The sum of the angles in a triangle is \(180^\circ\), and since \(CD \parallel AB\), \(\angle DCB = \angle ABC = 40^\circ\). Therefore, \(\angle BAC = 180^\circ - 2 \cdot 40^\circ = 100^\circ\). Since \(CD \parallel AB\), \(\angle ECD = \angle ACB = \frac{1}{2} (180^\circ - \angle BAC) = \frac{1}{2} (180^\circ - 100^\circ) = 40^\circ\). </think> <answer>40</answer>

Since \(AC = BC\), \(\Delta ABC\) is isosceles with \(\angle BAC = \angle ABC\). The sum of the angles in \(\Delta ABC\) is \(180^\circ\), so \(2 \angle BAC + \angle ACB = 180^\circ\). Since \(CD \parallel AB\) and \(\angle DCB = 40^\circ\), \(\angle ECD = \angle ACB = 40^\circ\) because they are corresponding angles. Hence, \(m\angle ECD = 40^\circ\). </think> <answer> 40 </answer>

Since \(AC = BC\), \(\Delta ABC\) is isosceles with \(\angle BAC = \angle ABC\). The sum of the angles in \(\Delta ABC\) is \(180^\circ\), so \(\angle BAC + \angle ABC + \angle ACB = 180^\circ\). Since \(\angle ACB = 40^\circ\), we have \(\angle BAC + \angle ABC + 40^\circ = 180^\circ\), which simplifies to \(\angle BAC + \angle ABC = 140^\circ\). Since \(\angle BAC = \angle ABC\), we have \(2 \times \angle BAC = 140^\circ\), so \(\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 70^\circ\). Since \(CD \parallel AB\) and \(AC\) is a transversal, \(\angle DCB = \angle ABC = 40^\circ\). The sum of the angles on a straight line is \(180^\circ\), so \(\angle ACD = 180^\circ - \angle ACB - \angle DCB = 180^\circ - 40^\circ - 40^\circ = 100^\circ\). Since \(\angle ACD = 100^\circ\) and \(\angle ACB = 40^\circ\), \(\angle ECD = \angle ACD - \angle DCB = 100^\circ - 40^\circ = 60^\circ\). </think> <answer> 60 </answer>

Since \(AC = BC\), \(\Delta ABC\) is isosceles with \(\angle BAC = \angle ABC\). The sum of the interior angles of \(\Delta ABC\) is \(180^\circ\). Therefore, \(\angle BAC + \angle ABC + \angle ACB = 180^\circ\). Given that \(\angle ACB = \angle DCB + \angle DCE = 40^\circ + \angle DCE\) and \(\angle DCB = 40^\circ\), we have \(\angle ABC = \angle ACB = \frac{180^\circ - (40^\circ + \angle DCE)}{2}\). Since \(CD \parallel AB\), \(\angle DCB = \angle BAC = 40^\circ + \angle ECD\). Thus, \(x + CE = 40^\circ + x\), and solving for \(x\) gives \(x = 20^\circ\). </think> <answer>20</answer>

Since \(AC = BC\), \(\Delta ABC\) is isosceles with \(\angle BAC = \angle ABC\). Given that \(CD \parallel AB\), \(\angle DCB\) is equal to \(\angle ABC\) because they are corresponding angles. So, \(\angle ABC = 40^\circ\). Since \(\angle BAC = \angle ABC\) and \(\angle BAC + \angle ABC + \angle ACB = 180^\circ\) in \(\Delta ABC\), \(\angle BAC = 40^\circ\). Consequently, \(\angle ACB = 180^\circ - 40^\circ - 40^\circ = 100^\circ\). Since \(CD \parallel AB\), \(\angle DCB = \angle ABC = 40^\circ\), and because \(CD\) is a straight line, \(\angle ACD = 180^\circ - \angle ACB = 180^\circ - 100^\circ = 80^\circ\). Thus, \(\angle ECD = \angle ACD - \angle DCB = 80^\circ - 40^\circ = 40^\circ\). </think> <answer> 40 </answer>
